questions.q: What does the `getStaticProps` function do in Next.js?
options: Fetches data on every request at runtime
Fetches data at build time and passes it as props to the page
Fetches data only on the client side
Fetches data after the page has loaded

questions.q: Which Next.js router hook gives you access to the current pathname?
options: useRoute
usePathname
useRouter
useNavigation

questions.q: In Next.js App Router, what is a Server Component by default?
options: Components inside the `pages/` directory
All components inside the `app/` directory
Components marked with `export default`
Components using React hooks

questions.q: How do you define a dynamic route segment in Next.js?
options: Using `[param].js` file naming convention
Using `{param}.js` file naming convention
Using `:param.js` file naming convention
Using `<param>.js` file naming convention

questions.q: What is the purpose of `next/image` component?
options: To add CSS styling to images
To provide automatic image optimization, lazy loading, and responsive images
To convert images to SVG format
To upload images to a CDN

questions.q: What does `getServerSideProps` do differently from `getStaticProps`?
options: It runs only once at build time
It runs on the client side after hydration
It runs on every request at runtime, enabling server-side rendering
It caches data indefinitely

questions.q: Which file is used to customize the default `<html>` and `<body>` tags in the Pages Router?
options: `_app.js`
`index.js`
`_document.js`
`layout.js`

questions.q: In Next.js, what is Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR)?
options: A way to render pages incrementally during initial load
A feature that allows static pages to be updated after build by revalidating at a specified interval
A method to load JavaScript incrementally
A technique for lazy-loading components

questions.q: How do you create an API route in the Next.js Pages Router?
options: Create a file in the `api/` folder at the root
Create a file inside `pages/api/` directory
Create a file in the `routes/` directory
Export a function named `apiHandler` from any page

questions.q: What is the purpose of the `'use client'` directive in the App Router?
options: It marks a file for server-side caching
It enables TypeScript in the component
It marks a component as a Client Component, enabling browser APIs and React hooks
It enables CSS modules for the component

questions.q: What does the `next.config.js` file control?
options: TypeScript configuration
Next.js build and server behavior, including custom headers, redirects, rewrites, and environment settings
Tailwind CSS settings
ESLint rules

questions.q: Which rendering strategy does a page use when it exports `getStaticProps` without `getStaticPaths`?
options: Server-Side Rendering (SSR)
Client-Side Rendering (CSR)
Static Site Generation (SSG) for a single static page
Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR)

questions.q: What does `getStaticPaths` return?
options: A list of CSS files to include
An array of pre-defined paths to statically generate for dynamic routes
A list of environment variables
The fallback rendering strategy only

questions.q: What does the `fallback: 'blocking'` option in `getStaticPaths` do?
options: Shows a loading spinner for new paths
Returns 404 for any path not in `paths`
Server-renders new paths on first request, then caches them as static
Blocks all navigation until all paths are loaded

questions.q: How do you programmatically navigate to another page in the App Router?
options: Using `window.location.href`
Using `useRouter` from 'next/navigation' and calling `router.push()`
Using `Link` component only
Using `redirect()` from React

questions.q: What is a Route Handler in Next.js App Router?
options: A middleware function that runs before every page
A special file (`route.js`) inside the `app/` directory that handles HTTP requests
A component that wraps all routes
A config file for defining route aliases

questions.q: What is the role of `layout.js` in Next.js App Router?
options: It defines the global CSS styles
It wraps child pages/layouts and persists across route changes without re-rendering
It handles data fetching for all pages
It configures the database connection

questions.q: How do you handle environment variables in Next.js that should be accessible on the client side?
options: Store them in `.env.local` with any name
Prefix them with `NEXT_PUBLIC_` in your `.env` file
Add them to `next.config.js` under `serverEnv`
Use `process.env.CLIENT_*` naming

questions.q: What does the `<Link>` component in Next.js do compared to a regular `<a>` tag?
options: It adds CSS transitions between pages
It enables client-side navigation without a full page reload and prefetches linked pages
It creates external hyperlinks only
It validates that routes exist before rendering

questions.q: What is middleware in Next.js used for?
options: Adding global CSS
Running code before a request is completed, enabling authentication, redirects, and request modification
Managing database connections
Handling form submissions

questions.q: What is a catch-all route in Next.js?
options: A route that handles all 404 errors
A dynamic route using `[...slug].js` that matches multiple path segments
A route that catches JavaScript errors
A fallback route defined in `_app.js`

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a valid way to fetch data in Next.js App Router Server Components?
options: Using the native `fetch()` API with caching options
Using an ORM directly in the component
Using `useEffect` with `fetch`
Using a database client directly

questions.q: What does `revalidate` in `getStaticProps` control?
options: How often the component re-renders on the client
The number of seconds after which a static page regenerates in the background (ISR)
How often API routes are called
The session timeout duration

questions.q: How can you share state between a parent Server Component and a child Client Component?
options: Using React Context in the Server Component
By passing data as props from the Server Component to the Client Component
Using Redux across both component types
Using `useServerState` hook

questions.q: What is the `loading.js` file in the App Router?
options: A script for lazy-loading JavaScript bundles
A special file that creates a loading UI shown while a route segment loads using React Suspense
A configuration file for webpack loading rules
A component for a global loading spinner

questions.q: What is the purpose of `error.js` in the App Router?
options: It logs errors to a remote server
It defines a React error boundary that catches runtime errors in a route segment
It handles HTTP 404 errors globally
It validates form inputs

questions.q: What is a Parallel Route in Next.js App Router?
options: Routes that run simultaneously on the server
A way to simultaneously render multiple pages in the same layout using `@folder` conventions
Routes that load JavaScript in parallel
API routes that handle concurrent requests

questions.q: How do you redirect a user in a Server Component or Route Handler in Next.js App Router?
options: `useRouter().push('/path')`
`window.location.href = '/path'`
`redirect('/path')` from 'next/navigation'
`<Redirect to='/path' />`

questions.q: What is the `notFound()` function used for in Next.js App Router?
options: It returns a custom 500 error page
It triggers the `not-found.js` file to render a 404 response for the current route segment
It checks if a route exists before navigating
It removes a route from the build

questions.q: Which of the following best describes Server Actions in Next.js?
options: Functions that run exclusively in the browser
Async functions marked with 'use server' that execute server-side code from Client Components or forms
Built-in Next.js API routes for CRUD operations
WebSocket event handlers on the server

questions.q: What does the `cache` option `'no-store'` do when passed to `fetch` in a Server Component?
options: Caches the response indefinitely
Revalidates the cache every 60 seconds
Opts the fetch out of caching, making it dynamic on every request
Stores data in localStorage

questions.q: What is `next/font` used for?
options: Loading third-party icon libraries
Automatically self-hosting fonts with zero layout shift and optimized performance
Adding Google Analytics font tracking
Converting fonts to WOFF2 format

questions.q: What is the difference between `pages/404.js` and `app/not-found.js`?
options: There is no difference
`pages/404.js` is for the Pages Router, while `not-found.js` is the App Router's equivalent for custom 404 pages
`app/not-found.js` handles server errors
`pages/404.js` handles API errors only

questions.q: How does Next.js handle code splitting?
options: Manually, requiring developers to configure webpack
Automatically, splitting code per page/route so only the necessary JavaScript is loaded
Using a single bundle for the entire application
Only splitting CSS files automatically

questions.q: What is the `Suspense` boundary used for in Next.js App Router?
options: Handling HTTP request timeouts
Wrapping asynchronous components to show fallback UI while they load, enabling streaming
Suspending API rate limiting
Pausing server actions

questions.q: What does `revalidatePath` do in Next.js?
options: Validates the URL format of a given path
Purges the cached data for a specific path and triggers regeneration on the next request
Checks if a path exists in the file system
Refreshes CSS styles for a route

questions.q: What is `revalidateTag` used for in Next.js?
options: Revalidating HTML meta tags
Purging cache entries associated with a specific cache tag set during fetching
Refreshing component props with a tag name
Resetting React state with a key

questions.q: In Next.js, what is an Intercepting Route?
options: A route that blocks navigation until authentication is verified
A route that intercepts another route's rendering context to show it modally, e.g., in a modal overlay
A middleware that intercepts all API calls
A route guard for protected pages

questions.q: What is the difference between `generateStaticParams` and `getStaticPaths`?
options: They are identical functions
`generateStaticParams` is the App Router equivalent of `getStaticPaths`, used with dynamic segments in the `app/` directory
`generateStaticParams` only works for SSR
`getStaticPaths` works in both routers

questions.q: What is the `useFormStatus` hook used for in Next.js?
options: Tracking the status of React state updates
Providing the pending state and other status info of a parent form submission, useful with Server Actions
Managing HTTP response status codes
Checking if a Server Component has finished rendering

questions.q: What does `useOptimistic` do in Next.js with Server Actions?
options: Optimizes image loading automatically
Allows showing optimistic UI updates immediately while a Server Action is in progress
Pre-fetches routes optimistically
Reduces bundle size by tree-shaking

questions.q: What does the `generateMetadata` function do in the App Router?
options: Generates sitemap.xml automatically
Dynamically generates SEO metadata (title, description, OG tags) for a page based on route params or fetched data
Creates JSON-LD structured data
Generates robots.txt rules

questions.q: Which of the following correctly sets `Cache-Control` headers in a Next.js Route Handler?
options: `res.cache('max-age=60')`
Returning a `Response` object with `headers: { 'Cache-Control': 's-maxage=60' }`
Setting it in `next.config.js` headers array only
Using `setCacheControl()` from next/server

questions.q: What happens when you add a `default.js` file in a parallel route slot?
options: It becomes the homepage for that slot
It serves as a fallback rendered when Next.js cannot determine a slot's active state during a soft navigation
It defines default export behavior for the route
It disables the parallel route feature

questions.q: What is `next/dynamic` used for?
options: Fetching dynamic data from an API
Dynamically importing components with lazy loading, optionally disabling SSR for that component
Creating dynamic environment variables
Generating dynamic routes at runtime

questions.q: Which file must exist at the root of the `app/` directory in the App Router?
options: `index.js`
`_app.js`
`layout.js` (root layout)
`page.js`

questions.q: What is the purpose of `template.js` vs `layout.js` in the App Router?
options: They are identical in behavior
`template.js` re-mounts on every navigation, creating a new instance, while `layout.js` persists
`template.js` is used for API routes
`template.js` handles authentication templates

questions.q: How do you access route parameters in a Next.js App Router page component?
options: Using `useRouter().query`
Via the `params` prop passed to the page component by Next.js
Using `getParams()` from 'next/navigation'
Via `this.props.match.params`

questions.q: What does `unstable_cache` from `next/cache` provide?
options: An experimental CSS caching solution
A way to cache the result of async functions (like database queries) similar to fetch caching
An unstable version of ISR
Client-side caching of React state

questions.q: Which of the following is true about the App Router's data fetching model?
options: Each component must declare its data dependencies in a global store
Data fetching is co-located in the components that need it; multiple components can fetch the same data and Next.js deduplicates requests
All data must be fetched in the root layout and passed down as props
Data fetching is only possible in Client Components
